build_stage: Stage 0 — constraint manifest audit (prerequisite for Stage 1 validator)

codebase_pinning.narrative_drift_note: CLAUDE.md narrative most-recent EXPECTED is 2877; bank probe reads 2883 (+6). Recent LATEST-N entries declare 'EXPECTED unchanged' for paper-side packs; the +6 likely landed in unrelated module work since LATEST-44.

purpose: Structural reference for the constraint cascade that determines admissible flow configurations on the (61, 102) lattice. Stage 1 validator reads this manifest and produces ValidationReport instances. Downstream engineering-tool work uses this manifest as the type specification for what the framework constrains.
interpretation_of_lattice: The (61, 102) lattice IS the second-epsilon commitment space (per check_T_horizon_reciprocity + check_T_interface_sector_bridge). Cells are not abstract index pairs — each (i, j) is a commitment proposition: 'channel i commits its 2nd-ε to target j.' Flow values represent the rate/probability of that commitment being active. The constraints below are admissibility rules on the commitment structure.

lattice.interpretation: Each cell (i, j) represents channel i's second-epsilon commitment to target j. Row i indexes an enforcement channel; column j indexes a commitment target. Flow value at (i, j) ∈ [0, 1] is the probability/rate of i committing 2nd-ε to j. Row sums equal 1 (each channel spends exactly 2ε). Sector A (cols 0–59) = partner-channel targets within V_61 (excluding self). Sector B (cols 60–101) = vacuum-mode targets in V_global.

partition.interpretation: Dark capacity role (Ω_D, APF-CDM under minimal gate: gravitationally coupled, SM-gauge-silent, collisionless)
Vacuum / cosmological role (Ω_Λ). V_global subspace; same 42-dim object that appears as Sector B target space for any channel.
Specific partner-channel commitments (bilateral matching targets within V_61, excluding self). 60 = 61 - 1 via L_self_exclusion.
Unilateral commitments to vacuum modes in V_global (the 42-dim global-interface stratum from T12). Equivalent at dS horizon to horizon-absorber stratum (L_global_interface_is_horizon).

constraints.rule_summary: Total enforcement capacity is finite (Axiom A1).
constraints.lattice_form: Sum over all flow values is bounded by total capacity. In the normalized form (row sums = 1), this is automatically satisfied; in unnormalized units it imposes a global ceiling.

constraints.rule_summary: Minimum enforceable distinction: non-zero flows have a minimum positive value ε*.
constraints.lattice_form: For each cell (i, j): flow[i, j] = 0 OR flow[i, j] ≥ ε*. Intermediate values are inadmissible.

constraints.rule_summary: Admissibility judgment: flow on inadmissible (channel, target) pairs must be zero.
constraints.lattice_form: For each cell (i, j): if A2 judges (i, j) inadmissible, flow[i, j] = 0.
constraints.failure_mode: Non-zero flow on a cell A2 rules inadmissible

constraints.rule_summary: Bounded weight: per-cell intensity is capped by the PLEC bounded-weight commitment.
constraints.lattice_form: For each cell (i, j): flow[i, j] ≤ 1 in the normalized form.

bank_witnesses.note: BW is one of the four PLEC commitments inside Regime R well-posedness

constraints.rule_summary: PLEC commitments A1/MD/A2/BW are derived consequences of the 4-input spine + finite-physical-regime hypothesis.
constraints.lattice_form: Not a per-cell rule; this is the deductive anchor that justifies the lattice's existence as a structurally-typed object.

constraints.rule_summary: Row count is rigidly 61 (= 45 chiral fermions + 4 Higgs components + 12 gauge generators).
constraints.lattice_form: rows = 61, immutable. Adding or removing a row produces a non-APF lattice.

constraints.rule_summary: Column count is 102 = 60 (Sector A: bilateral partner-targets) + 42 (Sector B: unilateral V_global targets).
constraints.lattice_form: cols = 102 with the 60+42 partition fixed by the second-epsilon commitment structure.
constraints.failure_mode: Lattice with cols ≠ 102 or wrong sector split

constraints.rule_summary: Channel i cannot commit its 2nd-ε to itself (self-correlation excluded from microstate counting).
constraints.lattice_form: Diagonal of Sector A submatrix must be zero. In the conventional indexing where Sector A column j maps to channel j (j < 60) and channel i ≥ j contributes flow to column j when committing to channel j, the self-cell flow[i, i_under_map] = 0.
constraints.failure_mode: Non-zero diagonal entries on the Sector A submatrix under the canonical channel→column map

constraints.rule_summary: Sector A commitments are obligatorily symmetric: i ↔ j implies j ↔ i (T_kappa + L_irr).
constraints.lattice_form: Under the canonical channel→column map on Sector A: flow[i, j_under_map] = flow[j, i_under_map]. The Sector A submatrix is symmetric.

constraints.rule_summary: T_M monogamy: each channel participates in at most one independent Sector A correlation.
constraints.lattice_form: For each row i: at most one non-zero entry in cols 0–59 (Sector A).
constraints.failure_mode: Row with multiple non-zero Sector A entries

constraints.rule_summary: Sector B commitments target the V_global vacuum-mode subspace.
constraints.lattice_form: Cols 60–101 index V_global modes; any non-zero Sector B cell encodes a commitment to the corresponding vacuum mode.

constraints.rule_summary: The 42 vacuum rows (capacity partition) and the 42 Sector B columns (commitment-target partition) index the SAME 42-dim subspace V_global. Two readings of one geometric object.
constraints.lattice_form: The 42×42 block at (rows: 19–60) × (cols: 60–101) is governed by a single index map: vacuum-row-(19+k) and Sector-B-col-(60+k) refer to the same V_global mode for k=0..41. Off-diagonal flow in this block represents inter-vacuum-mode coupling; the geometric identity forbids treating row-vacuum and col-vacuum as independent.
constraints.failure_mode: Flow configuration that treats vacuum-row index and Sector-B-col index as independent V_global modes

constraints.rule_summary: Each channel spends exactly 2ε: first-ε for own existence (Γ_S), second-ε for environment record (Γ_E).
constraints.lattice_form: Sum of flow[i, :] across all 102 columns = 1 (normalized 2ε capacity).

constraints.rule_summary: Holographic identity: boundary entropy S_BH = A/(4 ℓ_P^2) = ln(dim H_horizon) = ACC_horizon. Bulk vacuum capacity equals boundary state-count log.
constraints.lattice_form: Sum over the vacuum-row × Sector-B-col block (the 42×42 V_global self-block) is constrained by the holographic identity to match the boundary capacity log.

constraints.rule_summary: Gauge-cosmological identity: K (π_F) = 61 integer = denominator (π_C) at the SM. The same integer 61 governs SM interface counts and cosmological capacity fractions.
constraints.lattice_form: The row count 61 and the capacity-fraction denominator 61 are necessarily the same integer (not coincidentally equal).

constraints.rule_summary: Thermo-quantum identity: S_vN(ρ_max_mixed) = ln(dim H) = ACC.
constraints.lattice_form: At maximum mixing (uniform flow distribution), the von Neumann entropy of the lattice equals the ACC log-dimension.

constraints.rule_summary: Action-thermo identity: lim_{β → 0} ln Z(β) = ACC.
constraints.lattice_form: The high-temperature partition function log converges to the ACC count.

constraints.rule_summary: All four unification identities I1, I2, I3, I4 hold jointly.
constraints.lattice_form: Joint consistency: any admissible flow configuration satisfies I1+I2+I3+I4 simultaneously.

constraints.rule_summary: Capacity partition rigidity: 3 + 16 + 42 = 61 is the unique admissible partition under the framework's constraints.
constraints.lattice_form: Row-block sizes are rigidly 3, 16, 42; no other partition of 61 is admissible.

constraints.rule_summary: Visible-sector channels carry SU(3) × SU(2) × U(1) gauge structure (the unique admissible template).
constraints.lattice_form: Rows 0–2 carry the gauge-template structure; admissible flows respect the cubic anomaly equation.

constraints.rule_summary: Visible sector contains the 45 fermion DoF (1 of 4680 admissible configurations).
constraints.lattice_form: Row-content within visible sector includes 45 fermion contributions + 4 Higgs + 12 gauge.

constraints.rule_summary: Gauge anomaly cancellation holds across visible sector.
constraints.lattice_form: Anomaly-cancellation conditions on flows within rows 0–2.

constraints.rule_summary: Weinberg angle: source sin²θ_W = 3/13; four-channel effective continuation sin²θ_eff = 3/13 + 4/5063 = 0.231559.
constraints.lattice_form: EW channel couplings within visible sector produce the 3/13 source identity at the interface; the +4/5063 lift arises from the four-channel record decomposition.

constraints.rule_summary: Dark sector exports as APF-CDM under minimal gate: gravitationally coupled, SM-gauge-silent, collisionless, particle-ID guarded-by-design.
constraints.lattice_form: Rows 3–18 carry vanishing Sector A coupling to visible-row gauge generators (SM-gauge-silent); admissible Sector A flow is restricted to dark↔dark and dark↔gravity couplings.

constraints.rule_summary: Vacuum sector exports as w(z) with APF2 second-order finite-continuability response w_2(x) = -1 + 3/61 - (16/61)x - (21/8)x(x − 1/3)(1−x) under gate G_2FCR. Zeroth-order baseline w = -1.
constraints.lattice_form: Vacuum-row × Sector-B-col self-block flow evolves under w_2(x) when the framework is read in cosmological time.

constraints.rule_summary: Vacuum stratum V_global ≡ dS horizon absorber subspace. Vacuum-row × Sector-B-col block ≡ horizon microstate count.
constraints.lattice_form: At the dS horizon, the 42×42 vacuum self-block computes S_dS via M(61, 42) bulk vs 102^61 horizon.

constraints.rule_summary: Lovelock prerequisites A9.1–A9.5 unified: Einstein form is the unique 4D admissible curvature law. Gravity is GR-limit geometry; corrections enter on the source side (T_vis + T_D + T_2FCR + T_rad).
constraints.lattice_form: Cross-sector coupling between visible+dark+vacuum rows obeys the Bianchi identity on the source-decomposed stress tensor.

constraints.rule_summary: Higgs-like scalar existence forced by EW pivot (broken vacuum + positive curvature → massive scalar).
constraints.lattice_form: Within visible sector, four cells correspond to Higgs DoF; admissible flow requires non-trivial broken-vacuum configuration.

constraints.rule_summary: Cross-interface residual κ_int(S) is two-sided bounded by explicit substrate-derived quantities (lower from MD/BW unconditional, upper from kernel-norm + far-separation exponential suppression under C1–C5).
constraints.lattice_form: When evaluating cross-interface flow envelopes (couplings between distinct interface instances of the lattice), the inter-lattice residual is rigidly bounded above and below.

constraints.rule_summary: Target codomain (scheme) is named with conventional definition (e.g., MSbar(μ), OS-W, pole, w(z)).
constraints.lattice_form: When a lattice configuration is promoted to a scheme-export observable via a codomain-transport route, this gate must close. Lattice admissibility alone does not entail export admissibility — Sector A/B flows must also pass G1–G6.

constraints.rule_summary: Composition of operations source → target is APF-internal or named external with provenance.
constraints.lattice_form: When a lattice configuration is promoted to a scheme-export observable via a codomain-transport route, this gate must close. Lattice admissibility alone does not entail export admissibility — Sector A/B flows must also pass G1–G6.

constraints.rule_summary: External constants and evaluators are listed with provenance (e.g., RunDec, FLAG, DIZET, Cobaya likelihoods).
constraints.lattice_form: When a lattice configuration is promoted to a scheme-export observable via a codomain-transport route, this gate must close. Lattice admissibility alone does not entail export admissibility — Sector A/B flows must also pass G1–G6.

constraints.rule_summary: Uncertainty propagated through route; residual envelope adjudicated against declared envelope.
constraints.lattice_form: When a lattice configuration is promoted to a scheme-export observable via a codomain-transport route, this gate must close. Lattice admissibility alone does not entail export admissibility — Sector A/B flows must also pass G1–G6.

constraints.rule_summary: Kernel composition does not reference the target value as input.
constraints.lattice_form: When a lattice configuration is promoted to a scheme-export observable via a codomain-transport route, this gate must close. Lattice admissibility alone does not entail export admissibility — Sector A/B flows must also pass G1–G6.

constraints.rule_summary: Residual against measurement is attributed to named route/scheme/covariance channels.
constraints.lattice_form: When a lattice configuration is promoted to a scheme-export observable via a codomain-transport route, this gate must close. Lattice admissibility alone does not entail export admissibility — Sector A/B flows must also pass G1–G6.

constraints.rule_summary: Every codomain-transport instance declares codomain_type ∈ {CODOMAIN_NUMERICAL, CODOMAIN_STRUCTURAL}.
constraints.lattice_form: Export routes attached to the lattice must declare whether they target a numerical or structural codomain.

constraints.rule_summary: 10 codomain-transport instances registered with 9-field certificates; PORTAL_CLASS_ROUTES frozenset enumerates portal-class routes (LATEST-74).
constraints.lattice_form: The 32-route Scheme Export Registry is the human-readable view; the bank schema is the type system.

gaps_in_lattice_expressibility.description: Helicities, polarizations, and propagation degrees of freedom are KINEMATIC and do not contribute to capacity-channel count (check_L_count Step 3). The (61, 102) lattice represents STRUCTURAL enforcement, not kinematic motion.
gaps_in_lattice_expressibility.implication: Lattice does not directly carry propagation dynamics — those live in the action-internalization layer (Paper 7). A full engineering tool may need a second lattice for the kinematic layer, coupled to this one through Paper 7.

gaps_in_lattice_expressibility.description: A1 (finite capacity) is a global sum bound. ACC = K ln(d_eff) is a global accounting identity. I1–I4 unification identities are cross-sector. These constraints don't pinpoint specific cells but constrain configurations as wholes.
gaps_in_lattice_expressibility.implication: Validator must compute global invariants (sums, ratios, von Neumann entropy at max mixing, partition function limit) alongside per-cell rules and report violations at the configuration level, not the cell level.

gaps_in_lattice_expressibility.description: The cross-interface residual κ_int(S) is bounded structurally (two-sided rigidity) but its exact value within the bounds isn't predicted — the saturation regime is an open empirical question.
gaps_in_lattice_expressibility.implication: Cross-interface flow couplings have an envelope, not a point value. The validator should accept any value inside the envelope as admissible and surface where the envelope itself is the constraint.

gaps_in_lattice_expressibility.description: Dark-sector w(z) is at [P_internal_2FCR + P_DESI+CMB+DESY5_95]; robust empirical P pending MCMC + DESI gates 3/4 (full-shape exact runtime + full-growth likelihood).
gaps_in_lattice_expressibility.implication: Vacuum-sector flow constraints have an empirical-promotion queue. The validator should mark vacuum-row flow predictions with their current export status, not treat them as fully closed.

gaps_in_lattice_expressibility.description: The current lattice represents one interface (one capacity-allocation snapshot). Dynamics ACROSS multiple interfaces (e.g., how matter at one interface couples to matter at another) require a multi-lattice / interface-graph representation, not captured in (61, 102) alone.
gaps_in_lattice_expressibility.implication: Stage 1 validator handles single-interface admissibility. Multi-interface coupling is a Stage 2+ object that requires an extended representation (lattice × graph).

gaps_in_lattice_expressibility.description: The (61, 102) lattice is SM-specific: 61 = 45+4+12 is the SM channel count, 42 is the V_global dimension at the SM cosmological partition. For non-SM engineered systems (QEC codes, communication channels, distributed systems), the analog lattice dimensions would differ.
gaps_in_lattice_expressibility.implication: Stage 3 (engineering generalization) requires re-deriving the lattice dimensions and constraint cascade for each target engineered system. The Stage 0 manifest is the template, not the instance.

stage_1_validator_design_notes: Read this manifest as the constraint rule-set. For each entry with category=foundational/structural/coupling, implement a check function that takes a (61, 102) numpy array and returns ConstraintResult{passed, violations_at_cells, details}.
Sector-specific entries can be lifted to validator functions when the user wants per-sector validation; not required for v0.1 validator (only the cross-cutting rules are mandatory for admissibility).
Export gates (G1–G6) are per-route, not per-cell; the validator's export mode takes a route name plus the lattice configuration and checks the 6 gates against the route's existing certificate.

Global constraints (A1, ACC unification, I1–I4) require computing configuration-level invariants. Implementation: compute these as sum/log/entropy reductions over the lattice; check against the bank-witnessed expected values.
MD floor + BW ceiling are per-cell and trivial to check.

Sector A bilateral + monogamy require the canonical channel→column index map. The map is: column j in Sector A (j ∈ [0, 59]) corresponds to channel f(j) where f handles self-exclusion; in the cleanest convention f(j) = j for j < i, and (j+1) for j >= i, parameterized by row i. The validator implementation must fix this convention explicitly.
deductive_cascade: → Structural identities: L_count, L_self_exclusion, T_horizon_reciprocity, T_interface_sector_bridge
→ Sector-specific content: gauge template, sin²θ_W, dark-sector minimal CDM, vacuum w_2(x)
